mapping.a_element: adaptive resolution molecular dynamics scheme allowing free exchange of particles between regions of different resolution
mapping.b_element: adaptive resolution molecular dynamics simulation allowing free exchange of molecules between open regions of different resolution

mapping.a_element: use of auxiliary 'switching' functions to change resolution (force-based vs. potential energy-based approaches)
mapping.b_element: coupling to an internal particle reservoir as a generalized grand canonical approach for resolution adjustment

mapping.a_element: potential energy-based approach for resolution switching
mapping.b_element: effective generalized grand canonical approach for resolution adjustment

brief_justification: While both papers address adaptive resolution molecular dynamics with particle exchange between regions, Paper A focuses on the conceptual limitations of a potential energy-based switching mechanism, whereas Paper B introduces a generalized grand canonical approach for resolution adjustment. The core machinery differs: Paper A critiques specific algorithmic designs (force vs. potential energy), while Paper B proposes a thermodynamic reservoir-based method, making the underlying mechanisms distinct.
